summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API, specifically the RTCPeerConnection component, introduced enhancements that help improve video and audio performance during real-time communications. One significant change is the implementation of new statistics for encoded keyframes in WebRTC sessions, which allows developers to better analyze and optimize video quality during calls. Additionally, by incorporating modern code checks using clang-tidy, the codebase is being refined to follow best practices, which can lead to smoother functionality and easier maintenance in the long run. These improvements are essential as they help ensure WebRTC continues to deliver high-quality and reliable video calling experiences.
